threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'move down inside!' like it’s a foreign language.
Caught the N88 last night; a guy was serenading the driver with a guitar. Everyone just sat there, half amused, half confused. Only in Clapham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids making a rap video on the corner by REWE while the old couple next door argued about their garden. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can take a midnight U-Bahn ride and share the train with a DJ spinning tunes on a portable deck. Only in Berlin do parties roll through the underground!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you’re the only one unfazed by a BART delay and still make it to Tacolicious before 8.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy in a tuxedo jump on the 47, still holding a champagne flute. Only in SF do fancy soirées collide with the daily grind.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into a bar and get a seat anywhere. Locals know to arrive early or risk standing the whole night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by how they still look for the metro maps on the walls instead of pulling them up on their phone, especially at Fontana.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You stop at every bus stop to look at the timetable like it's a magic 8-ball, but locals just know when to hop on.
Caught a night bus last week, two blokes debating the pros and cons of cat cafes—completely serious, like it was a matter of life and death.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the bus stop as a skate ramp while their parents waited for the bus—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar with a swimming pool in the basement. It's a dive for after-hours swims and late-night chats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they don’t flinch at the BART delays but still complain about how bad the food at La Taqueria has gotten.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Today, a guy in a tuxedo hopped on and started playing the accordion. Best commute accompaniment I’ve had in weeks—only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' means small plates. Here, we call it 'pica-pica'; locals spread out their meals over hours rather than rushing through.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell the newcomers by their reluctance to use the local language even when everyone else is chattering away in Catalan at the café.
